title: Smoke first
description: A compressed desert artifact where morning weed lowers the gnaw but disturbs the day's motion, muting social contact and making church feel visibly strange.

motifMap.meaning: The morning smoke becomes a small ignition that cools the brain while displacing the day.
Freedom is present but turned sideways into drift, errands, snacks, and muted conversation.
The altered state becomes visible in a sacred-social setting, marking disturbance rather than relief.

excerpt: Smoke hot. Brain cool. The gnaw at bay. Whole day is mine to waste.

associatedMedia.description: Screenshot of the published Substack post page, if available in the local screenshot archive. Screenshot capture remains optional provenance support and was not required for validation in this run.
associatedMedia.altText: A short Wanderer's Burn Substack post titled 'Smoke first' by Unnamed, published May 11, 2026, describing morning weed, errands, muted conversation, and feeling weird at church.

editHistory.notes: Batch v2.6.5 metadata run. Classified as Desert Faith with Disturbed Motion because the post records a day diverted by smoke, muted contact, and disturbed ordinary practice.

integrity.notes: Filename: 2026-05-11.smoke-first.phase-desert-faith.variant-disturbed-motion.metadata.v2.6.5.json. Variant assignment follows v2.6.5 phase-variant compatibility.
